_comment: Quiet summer day — VIX at 12.68 against a calm declining window. Exercises the noise-zone axis: severity should be near zero, guarding against score inflation that would trigger false composite signals downstream.

expected_band.rationale_pending_trader: SRS CLS-001 ECDF: VIX median=14.54, |deviation|=|12.68-14.54|=1.86. Count of deviations ≤ 1.86: only 2 window values out of 20 have larger deviation (12.39 at 2.15, 12.57 at 1.97), so ecdf_rank=18, severity=18/20=0.90. This is the noise-zone axis: calm summer day with VIX drifting within its normal range. ECDF produces 0.90 because this is high relative to the calm window (top decile), yet it's not a tradeable signal — the z-score was negative (VIX below mean). Tanh gives 0.07, which incorrectly suggests signal strength below threshold. ECDF's 0.90 reflects that within this quiet regime, the move is relatively unusual, even if absolute level is tame.
